reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2
REASONING: It is Round 1 and there is no consensus on any of the answers yet. Volunteering with completely unverified capsules is extremely risky and often fatal, as I've experienced in previous games. Passing contributes nothing to the team's progress while still leaving a 25% chance of random elimination. Verifying Q2 only costs 1 coin, builds necessary consensus, and brings us closer to safely escaping in the upcoming rounds.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Since it's Round 1 and there is no consensus on any answers other than my own, volunteering now is too risky as I would have to rely on unverified answers from other players. Verifying Q1 helps build consensus, avoids the penalty of everyone passing without contributing, and makes a future volunteer attempt much safer.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
REASONING: In previous games, the puzzles have consistently been very simple math questions, meaning the likelihood of any player making a calculation error is extremely low (well under 1%). Conversely, if no one volunteers in this round, there is a 25% chance that I will be randomly eliminated. Volunteering immediately minimizes my overall risk of death and ensures our escape, saving everyone's coins and preventing any random eliminations.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.
